framing: AGUBC project allocations (May 17, 2026). 7 projects, 15,500 AF/yr total. Rock Creek recharge is now concentrated at 36P and 29P (3,500 AF/yr each); the prior 33A allocation was removed because Rock Creek recharge now lands at the two adjacent polygons rather than 33A.

projects.name: Comanche Creek recharge
projects.source: AGUBC allocation, May 2026 (carried from original portfolio)

projects.name: Rock Creek recharge
projects.source: AGUBC allocation, May 17, 2026 (increased from 2,000 to 3,500 as Rock Creek recharge re-sites away from 33A)

projects.name: Rock Creek recharge
projects.source: AGUBC allocation, May 17, 2026 (increased from 2,000 to 3,500 as Rock Creek recharge re-sites away from 33A)

notes: The two Chico cells in the new 28-set (22N01E09B001M and 22N01E20K001M) receive no project allocation. Their combined deficit is the largest remaining shortfall in the basin.

Project yields are at-average operating conditions. Recharge yields assume 2–3 storm events per year where excess flow can be diverted; drought-year yields will be lower, wet-year yields higher. Conjunctive-use yields vary with surface-water availability.

Rock Creek recharge previously included a 2,000 AF/yr allocation at 23N01E33A001M (May 15 portfolio). After field re-siting (May 17), Rock Creek recharge concentrates at the two polygons that physically front Rock Creek — 36P and 29P — at 3,500 AF/yr each. The 33A polygon now has no project allocation and reverts to a shortfall polygon.
